# Science & Technology

Scientific discoveries, technological launches, and digital developments from the year 2007.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->

| Topic | Summary |
|---|---|
| [iPhone Launch](iphone-launch.md) | Apple CEO Steve Jobs introduced the original iPhone on January 9 at Macworld; the device went on sale June 29, combining phone, internet browser, and iPod in one touchscreen device and transforming the smartphone industry. |
| [Amazon Kindle Launch](amazon-kindle-launch.md) | Amazon introduced the first Kindle e-reader on November 19 at $399; it sold out in 5.5 hours, launched with 90,000 titles, and marked the start of the mass-market e-reader era. |
| [Facebook Platform Launch](facebook-platform.md) | Mark Zuckerberg unveiled the Facebook Platform on May 24 at the first F8 developer conference, opening the social graph to third-party applications and establishing the open-platform model that fueled Facebook's rise past MySpace. |
| [Arctic Sea Ice Record Low and Northwest Passage Opening](arctic-sea-ice-record-low.md) | On September 16, Arctic sea ice extent fell to a satellite-era minimum of 4.13 million km²—about 24% below the 2005 record—and the Northwest Passage opened to navigation without icebreakers for the first time in recorded history. |
| [Induced Pluripotent Stem Cells Breakthrough](induced-pluripotent-stem-cells.md) | In November, the teams of Shinya Yamanaka (*Cell*) and James Thomson (*Science*) independently reprogrammed adult human cells into induced pluripotent stem cells, bypassing the embryonic-stem-cell ethical impasse and founding modern regenerative medicine. |
| [IPCC Fourth Assessment Report (AR4)](ipcc-fourth-assessment-report.md) | Released in four installments across 2007, the IPCC's most comprehensive climate assessment to that date concluded that warming is "unequivocal" and very likely (≥90%) human-caused; it shared the 2007 Nobel Peace Prize with Al Gore and framed the science underpinning the Bali Road Map. |
| [YouTube's Rise as Global Media Platform](youtube-growth-2007.md) | After its 2006 Google acquisition, YouTube reached 100+ million daily views and roughly 160 million users in 2007, launched its revenue-sharing Partner Program, co-hosted CNN's citizen-question presidential debates, and shipped on the original iPhone—laying the foundation of the creator economy. |
| [Google Street View Launch](google-street-view-launch.md) | Launched May 25, 2007, in five U.S. cities, Street View added panoramic street-level imagery to Google Maps and sparked lasting debates over geospatial data collection and personal privacy. |
| [Blu-ray vs. HD-DVD Format War](blu-ray-hd-dvd-format-war-2007.md) | The high-definition optical-disc format war reached its decisive phase in 2007 as PlayStation 3's installed base and exclusive studio and retailer commitments swung the market to Blu-ray over HD-DVD. |
| [Singularity University Founding](singularity-university-founding.md) | Peter Diamandis and Ray Kurzweil conceived Singularity University in mid-2007 to educate leaders in exponential technologies; it was formally established in 2008 at NASA Research Park and launched its first program in 2009. |

<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
